items.title: AT&S invests up to €2bil in Malaysia plan to capitalise on AI boom
items.summary: VIENNA: Austrian circuit board manufacturer AT&S has announced it is investing up to €2 billion (US$2.3 billion) in Malaysia to capitalise on the AI boom, sending its shares soaring nearly 30 per cent. "We will fully expand our site in Kulim," the company's chier executive officer Michael Mertin told Reuters in an interview on Monday. The company announced on Saturday it would expand capacity in Kulim and raised its outlook for 2026/27, citing agreements with its customer AMD, the US chipmaker, and another technology company on the back of strong demand for AI infrastructure.

items.title: MACC to charge individual with 158 offences linked to Op Sutra
items.summary: MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman said the individual would be charged under Section 23 of the MACC Act 2009. "The individual will also be charged next week at the Kuala Lumpur Sessions Court on several counts related to money laundering activities under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act (Amla)," he said. Also present was Sabah MACC director Datuk Mohd Fuad Bee Basrah.

items.title: MoF flags tender manipulation in govt procurement process
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Finance Ministry (MoF) has detected irregularities in government procurement, including tender specifications being tailored to favour specific vendors, says Treasury secretary-general Tan Sri Johan Mahmood Merican. He said although procurement was now largely conducted via open tender, there were cases where processes appeared competitive on paper but ultimately left only one qualified bidder after technical and financial evaluations due to skewed specifications. Johan said the Government Procurement Bill 2025 would strengthen accountability and enforcement.

items.title: Powerwell bags RM158.8mil data centre-related contract in Johor
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Powerwell Holdings Bhd's wholly owned subsidiary Kejuruteraan Powerwell Sdn Bhd has clinched a RM158.81 million contract to supply low voltage switchboards in connection with a data centre project in Johor. The electricity distribution product maker said its unit had received a duly executed supply contract dated June 11 from a leading international contractor for the supply and delivery. Powerwell added that the supply contract is expected to contribute positively to the group's consolidated earnings and net assets for the financial year ending March 31, 2027.

items.title: Transport Ministry to discuss e-hailing safety measures after assault cases
items.summary: SEPANG: The Transport Ministry will engage e-hailing operators on possible additional safety measures, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke. He said among the proposals raised was the installation of dashboard cameras in e-hailing vehicles to protect drivers. However, he said implementation must take into account passenger privacy concerns.

items.title: Vantris Energy returns to profit in Q1
items.summary: However, revenue fell to RM645.21 million in Q1 FY27, compared to RM801.37 million in Q1 FY26. "The decline was primarily attributable to lower contributions from the engineering and construction (E&C) segment, reflecting the group's deliberate strategic shift towards contracts with a more balanced risk profile across Southeast Asia and Australia, with a focus on lower-risk, service-oriented activities. The E&C segment is expected to remain resilient, supported by the recent completion of several major projects and a deliberate, strategic shift towards contracts with a more balanced risk profile.

items.title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher, tracking Asian peers on improved global sentiment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia's key index advanced at Monday's close, tracking gains across regional equities as geopolitical risk sentiment improved following an interim agreement between the United States and Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, said analysts. Turnover surged to 5.02 billion units worth RM3.91 billion from 2.79 billion units worth RM2.31 billion last Friday. IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said the US-Iran agreement has reduced near-term concerns over energy supply disruptions and lowered geopolitical risk premiums across global asset markets.

items.title: Loke: Cabinet greenlights Malaysia Transport Safety Board, staffing talks underway
items.summary: SEPANG, June 15 — The Cabinet has approved the establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB), with the Transport Ministry expecting to table the legislation in the next Parliament session, Anthony Loke said today. “The establishment of the Malaysia Transport Safety Board (MTSB) has already received Cabinet approval. Separately, Loke said the ministry plans to expand its Safe School Zone initiative after a pilot programme involving 14 schools nationwide.

items.title: Wawasan risks splitting opposition vote, weakening bloc – Expert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Wawasan Negara risks becoming a strategic liability if it ends up competing for the same voter base as other opposition parties instead of expanding its reach, says a political analyst. Universiti Malaya socio-political analyst Professor Dr Awang Azman Awang Pawi said the danger in Malay politics arose when multiple parties contested the same seats with near-identical narratives centred on Islam, Malay unity, stability and anti-government sentiment. He said the main beneficiary would be unity government parties, as a fragmented Malay opposition vote could allow government candidates to win with small pluralities.

items.title: 'Stop blaming Malaysia': Rights group demands Myanmar take accountability for Rohingya crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Rohingya crisis stems from Myanmar's own actions and policies, and responsibility for addressing it cannot be shifted to host countries such as Malaysia, the Centre for Human Rights Research and Advocacy (Centhra) said. Azril said enforcement efforts should focus more on human trafficking and smuggling syndicates, while Malaysia should consider developing a specific legal framework for refugee management. On international burden-sharing, Centhra said Malaysia could not continue carrying the humanitarian responsibility alone.

items.title: Sabah records 5.9pct drop in drug addiction cases, says Hajiji
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: Sabah recorded a 5.9 per cent drop in drug addiction cases, says Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. Sabah also recorded the lowest rate in the country last year, with 230 drug addicts and substance abusers detected. Hajiji also commended the National Anti-Drugs Agency for its community outreach efforts through grassroots prevention programmes.

items.title: Malaysia to discuss in-car cameras for e-hailing drivers after assault cases rise
items.summary: SEPANG, June 15 — The Transport Ministry is weighing new safeguards to shield e-hailing drivers from passenger violence, even as it doubles down on a strict crackdown against transport firms that flout safety standards. Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed that the ministry will enter talks with e-hailing operators to bolster driver security. “Passenger safety matters, and driver safety matters as well.

items.title: Loke: Safe School Zone pilot rolled out at 14 schools nationwide
items.summary: SEPANG: The Road Safety Council is rolling out a Safe School Zone pilot project at 14 schools nationwide, benefiting nearly 20,000 pupils and school staff. "This indicates that speed management measures and road infrastructure around many schools are still not at a satisfactory level," he said in his speech at the Road Safety Council's 62nd annual general meeting here today. Loke said the project focused on reducing vehicle speeds in school zones through infrastructure improvements, including signalised pedestrian crossings, road narrowing measures and pedestrian walkways.

items.title: Anwar: GLIC investment commitment in Bumiputera firms increased to RM2bil this year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investment commitments by government-linked investment companies (GLICs) in Bumiputera businesses have been increased to RM2 billion for 2026, compared with RM1.3 billion last year, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. In a post on social media platform X, he said the commitment includes the Bumiputera Relay Initiative (Larian Ganti Bumiputera), which aims to expand access to financing and investment for Bumiputera entrepreneurs at every stage of business growth. Anwar chaired the Second Meeting of the Bumiputera Economic Council (MEB) for 2026 today.

items.title: New RM650mil system to revolutionise Malaysia's healthcare delivery
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: A new RM650 million nationwide system is set to revolutionise healthcare services at government facilities, promising shorter waiting times, better care and seamless access to records for patients. Launched today, the National Digital Health Ecosystem and Connectivity Catalyst Initiative (Persada) will use a Cloud-Based Clinical Management System (CCMS) to enhance operational efficiency, improve doctor-patient care and streamline Malaysia's telehealth development. With Persada, the ministry is targeting 81 per cent of patients to receive treatment within an hour, said Dzulkefly.

items.title: 2026 BYD Atto 3 gets over 1,000 bookings in 10 days
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: BYD Sime Motors has secured more than 1,000 bookings for the newly launched 2026 BYD ATTO 3 within just 10 days of its introduction. In a statement, BYD Sime Motors said the achievement mirrors the model's Malaysian debut in 2022, when the BYD ATTO 3 reached the same booking milestone within an identical timeframe. "This strong start reaffirms the BYD ATTO 3's position as a favourite in Malaysia's EV landscape," he said.

items.title: Anthony Loke: LRT3 launch date to be announced in next two weeks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The long-awaited Light Rail Transit 3 (LRT3) line is ready to begin operations by the end of this month, with Transport Minister Anthony Loke saying the official launch date will be announced within the next two weeks. Speaking to reporters after the 62nd Annual General Meeting of the Malaysian Road Safety Council (MKJR) today, Loke said trial operations for the line have gone smoothly and the system is prepared for passenger service. “Trial operations have gone well and the system is ready.

items.title: Perodua slashes QV-E prices by nearly RM20,000, thanks to higher localisation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Higher localisation levels have enabled Perusahaan Otomobil Kedua Sdn Bhd (Perodua) to lower the price of its first battery electric vehicle (BEV), the QV-E. To help consumers manage rising living costs, Perodua is also offering a limited-time promotional price until Sept 30. The BaaS package includes battery protection, insurance, replacement and disposal services throughout the leasing period, giving customers peace of mind over battery ownership and maintenance.

items.title: Teacher remanded seven days from today in Kuala Terengganu over suspected sexual assault of 13-year-old boy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — A male teacher in his 30s has been remanded for seven days to assist investigations into allegations of physical sexual assault involving a 13-year-old boy, police said. According to Kuala Terengganu district police chief Asst Comm Azli Mohd Noor, a report was lodged by the victim at 9.38am yesterday. In a related development, ACP Azli said police in Kuala Terengganu opened four investigation papers involving sexual offences against children between June 1 and 14.

items.title: Bukit Kiara longhouse redevelopment to give 98 families free homes, part of land to be gazetted as public open space, says FT minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Minister in the Prime Minister’s Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh said several key principles have been agreed upon by all parties involved in the Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement and redevelopment project. Among the key terms, she said residents of the longhouse will receive replacement housing as previously promised, while development at the site will be strictly limited to 2.33 hectares. “The replacement housing will be constructed and completed first before residents are relocated.

items.title: No suspects yet in gruesome Tongod elephant killing as Sabah probe stalls over lack of evidence, says state minister
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, June 15 — Investigators have yet to identify any suspects in the gruesome killing of a Bornean elephant found decapitated in Tongod last month, with Sabah authorities saying the remote location of the crime and lack of evidence continue to hamper efforts to solve the case. State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said the investigation is still ongoing and authorities are waiting for it to be completed before drawing any conclusions. Jafry acknowledged the challenges faced by enforcement agencies, noting that Sabah's vast forested areas make wildlife monitoring a difficult task.

items.title: Selangor to roll out RM100 monthly aid for 50,000 families under Kita Selangor vouchers
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 15 — A total of 50,000 low-income families in Selangor will receive cash aid of RM100 a month for six months under the Kita Selangor Voucher initiative, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari announced today. He said the initiative, which will begin on June 30, is part of the RM140 million Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 unveiled on April 16 to help residents cope with global economic uncertainty, rising living costs and the impact of the global supply crisis. “Our target is for recipients to start receiving the RM100 monthly assistance from June 30, with payments continuing for six months,” he said after launching the package at the Selangor Economic Action Council (MTES) at the Selangor State Secretariat building here.

items.title: Foreign investors buy China's onshore yuan bonds in May, first time in over a year
items.summary: SHANGHAI: Foreign investors bought Chinese onshore yuan-denominated bonds in May for the first time since April 2025, official data showed on Monday. Foreign institutions held 3.21 trillion yuan (US$475 billion) in bonds traded on China's interbank market as of the end of May, the central bank's Shanghai head office said, up from 3.12 trillion yuan a month earlier. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Turkish-built command platform to replace Sabah's frontline sea base
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Tun Sharifah Rodziah Sea Base, which served as a frontline bastion safeguarding the security and sovereignty of the nation's waters in Eastern Sabah for nearly a decade, will be replaced by a Turkish-built Multi-Purpose Command Platform (MPCP). The National Security Council (NSC) said procurement of the MPCP was approved under the 13th Malaysia Plan and would be carried out through government-to-government (G2G) cooperation between Malaysia and Türkiye. The NSC described the MPCP as a game-changing asset that would significantly enhance surveillance capabilities along Eastern Sabah's maritime border.

items.title: REV bags seven awards at MDA d'Awards 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: REV Media Group (REV) secured seven awards at the Malaysian Digital Association's (MDA) d'Awards 2026, including Gold awards for Publisher of the Year and Content Agency of the Year. The company received three Gold and four Silver awards across categories spanning digital publishing, branded content, artificial intelligence (AI)-driven innovation, audience growth, integrated screens and talent development. The company also won Gold for Best Digital Growth Campaign, reflecting its efforts in audience expansion and digital platform performance.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Urban poor not sidelined, Bukit Kiara project proves rights upheld
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has never sidelined the rights of the urban poor in their pursuit of a better life, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said the Bukit Kiara longhouse permanent housing project was proof that the rights of urban poor communities could be upheld, even after more than four decades of waiting for proper homes. Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.

items.title: Bersatu blasts Samsuri for 'very irresponsible' leadership in PN crisis
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz has accused Perikatan Nasional (PN) chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar of failing to act in his capacity as coalition chief, saying he appeared to have forgotten that he was coalition chairman and not Pas chairman. Tun Faisal said the admission by Johor Pas commissioner Datuk Dr Mahfodz Mohamed that the party's decision to cut ties with Bersatu had affected preparations for the Johor state election - and that Johor Pas intended to use its own logo - was proof that Pas' actions were irresponsible and damaging to PN's election readiness. The remarks come after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said Datuk Hamzah Zainudin will be reappointed as opposition leader and said Pas would use the PN logo in the upcoming state elections.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: People suffer when politicians stir racial tensions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has urged Malaysians not to be swayed by campaigns aimed at dividing communities along racial lines. He said such efforts undermine national unity and ultimately harm ordinary people, whose concerns are often overshadowed by divisive politics. Also present were Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Federal Territories) Hannah Yeoh and Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan.

items.title: Govt allocates RM1m for Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement upkeep, DBKL to manage maintenance, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The government has approved RM1 million for residents involved in the Bukit Kiara longhouse resettlement and redevelopment project, with Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) tasked to manage maintenance of the housing scheme, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. Anwar said the allocation is intended to ease the financial burden on residents after relocation and ensure the long-term upkeep of the housing area is properly managed. “Later, DBKL will manage and oversee the maintenance arrangements so that monthly costs can be covered and residents are not burdened,” he said in his speech at the ground-breaking ceremony for permanent housing for Bukit Kiara longhouse residents here today.

items.title: Chow: Penang PH wants more women candidates, but still a challenge
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Penang Pakatan Harapan (PH) hopes to field more women candidates in the next state election, although identifying enough suitable and willing candidates remains a challenge, says its chairman Chow Kon Yeow. He said Penang PH remained committed to achieving at least 30 per cent women's participation in politics and decision-making, but continued to face difficulties in recruiting sufficient candidates. In every election, we will field women candidates, but the actual number depends on whether there are enough suitable and willing individuals.

items.title: UK PM announces ban on social media for under-16s
items.summary: LONDON: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Monday announced a forthcoming total ban on social media for children under 16, saying that such platforms are "making children unhappy." Starmer said that the government "will ban access to social media for all children under the age of 16", warning that these platforms are "exposing them to content that is dangerous" and "designed to be addictive." The prime minister also said the government would go further and take "world-leading action on gaming services and live streaming platforms."

items.title: ‘We’re monitoring, not panicking’: Amirudin says Selangor still on track for RM2.8b target
items.summary: Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government did not see the need to take emergency measures because revenue collection remains on a positive track. State revenue collection reached RM1.6 billion as of May against this year’s RM2.8 billion target, with the current collection trend showing positive development. Amirudin added that Selangor also recorded a RM300 million surplus against total expenditure due to the state government’s savings.

items.title: Special state assembly sitting not linked to polls, says Selangor MB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Selangor special state assembly sitting beginning Friday is not linked to the dissolution of the state assembly or any state election preparations, Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said today. Instead, Amirudin said the three-day sitting on June 19, 22 and 23 will focus on the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 2, aimed at reinforcing the state’s economic and social support measures, reported Harian Metro today. He said this at a press conference after launching the Selangor Resilience Strengthening Package Phase 1 at the Selangor State Economic Action Unit (MTES) meeting room in the state secretariat in Shah Alam today.

items.title: Selangor finances stay strong amid global headwinds, says MB
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Selangor's financial position remains healthy despite global economic uncertainties arising from supply disruptions and fluctuations in world markets. Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said the state government saw no need for emergency measures as revenue collection continued on a positive trajectory. Amirudin said the state also recorded a RM300 million surplus against expenditure due to prudent spending.

items.title: Op Tiris: GOF deployment deters petrol, diesel smuggling to Thailand
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The deployment of General Operations Force (GOF) personnel at petrol stations identified as smuggling hotspots along the Kelantan–Thailand border has deterred attempts to smuggle subsidised petrol and diesel across the border, said GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain. He said the inspections had curtailed smugglers' ability to obtain subsidised fuel freely and transport it across the border. Radzi said GOF had also refined its operational strategy by focusing on vehicles that repeatedly refuel at specific petrol stations.

items.title: Rosmah lodges report over RM55m US property claim, police say viral account likely fake
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Datin Seri Rosmah Mansor has lodged a police report over viral allegations linking her to the purchase or financing of a luxury property in New Jersey, United States, reportedly valued at RM55 million (US$13 million), Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. Tan Sri Mohd Khalid said preliminary investigations suggested the social media account behind the allegation was likely fake. “Preliminary investigations found that the social media account spreading the allegation is believed to be fake,” he said at a press conference during the launch of the Perangi Scam: Two Teams, One Goal campaign here.

items.title: Shares and bonds surge, oil slides on Iran deal
items.summary: LONDON/SYDNEY: Share markets and bonds rallied hard on Monday and oil prices tumbled five per cent as a framework peace deal between the United States and Iran promised to ease inflationary pressures globally and lessen the need for higher interest rates. Asia's biggest markets had all leapt overnight, while futures markets pointed to gains of 1.3 per cent to 2.0 per cent for Wall Street later. South Korea's red-hot market gained 5.2 per cent, and Chinese blue chips firmed 1.4 per cent.

items.title: Weather warning blankets nine states as MetMalaysia cautions of thunder, rain and strong winds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds for nine states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 6pm today. It said in a statement that, apart from the whole of Perlis, the adverse weather conditions are also expected to hit Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar and Pokok Sena); Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun); and Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin). Similar weather conditions are also expected in Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian and Kulai).

items.title: Thunderstorms, heavy rain to hit KL, nine states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds for nine states and the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur until 6pm today. It said in a statement that, apart from the whole of Perlis, the adverse weather conditions are also expected to hit Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar and Pokok Sena); Terengganu (Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu, Kuala Terengganu, Marang and Dungun); and Pahang (Raub, Bentong, Temerloh, Bera and Rompin). Similar weather conditions are also expected in Selangor (Hulu Selangor, Gombak and Hulu Langat); Negeri Sembilan (Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol and Tampin); and Johor (Segamat, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, Pontian and Kulai).

items.title: Two suspected scam operatives nabbed with multiple phones, SIM cards near border
items.summary: RANTAU PANJANG: Two Chinese nationals suspected of involvement in online scam activities were detained near the Malaysia-Thailand border after they were found in possession of dozens of mobile phones and foreign SIM cards. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the men, aged 32 and 34, were arrested in Kampung Siram near Jeram Perdah here early today. Radzi said a further search uncovered 45 mobile phones, 142 foreign SIM cards and two pen drives, with the items valued at about RM48,000.

items.title: ‘Drop‑off’ deliveries now traffickers’ weapon of choice, police warn couriers to stay sharp
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, June 15 — Kelantan police have called in more than 10 courier service companies operating in the state to share information on the latest modus operandi employed by drug syndicates, which have been found using parcel delivery channels to facilitate their activities. Kelantan police chief Datuk Mohd Yusoff Mamat said the initiative was intended to heighten vigilance among courier companies and their employees after numerous drug cases were found to involve courier services. He said courier firms had been asked to keep records of senders’ particulars, including details of their identity cards and phone numbers, to aid investigations should any offences arise.

items.title: IGP: 10,000 CCTV cameras will boost enforcement efforts in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The installation of 10,000 closed-circuit television (CCTV) cameras across Kuala Lumpur will strengthen policing and assist law enforcement efforts. Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said the expanded use of surveillance technology would help police overcome manpower limitations while improving operational efficiency. Khalid said technology, including information technology and artificial intelligence, should be further enhanced within the force to improve investigations and daily policing duties.

items.title: Special tribunal proposed to investigate alleged Rohingya smuggling network
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: Persatuan Melayu Pulau Pinang (Pemenang) has urged the government to establish a special tribunal to investigate allegations of a syndicate smuggling Rohingya refugees into Malaysia. Its president, Tan Sri Dr Mohd Yussof Latiff, said the alleged syndicate was not only exploiting vulnerable refugees but also eroding the country's dignity, security and sovereignty. He said the issue of Rohingya settlements in Malaysia had reached a critical stage, citing concerns over illegal settlements and increasing enforcement operations by the authorities.

items.title: Ahmad Maslan rules himself out of Johor race, says others should get the chance
items.summary: ISKANDAR PUTERI, June 15 — Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan has confirmed he will not contest in the upcoming Johor state election, despite speculation linking him to several state seats. Ahmad, who is also Deputy Works Minister, said he has no intention of contesting either the Benut or Pulai Sebatang state seat within the Pontian parliamentary seat. The Election Commission has set July 11 as polling day for the 16th Johor state election, with nomination day on June 27 and early voting on July 7.

items.title: Humanitarian approach towards Rohingya not a sign of weakness, says Perak MB
items.summary: IPOH, June 15 — Showing restraint when dealing with Rohingya refugees who violate local laws and regulations should not be mistaken for a sign of weakness, but rather as an expression of compassion and humanity, Perak Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Saarani Mohamad said. Saarani said Rohingya refugees holding United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) cards are subject to international law and should be treated appropriately while residing in Malaysia. He said the state government hoped the Rohingya community would understand and appreciate the humanitarian approach extended to them and refrain from violating local laws and regulations.

items.title: ‘We cannot manage what we do not know’: Saifuddin admits refugee data gap, pledges biometric fix
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, June 15 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail has called on academics, civil society activists, think tanks, legal practitioners and security experts to submit constructive proposals on refugee management in Malaysia, an issue he described as a longstanding legacy challenge. Saifuddin stressed that the Madani government was committed to handling the matter responsibly, prudently and based on facts, with clear priorities — safeguarding national security while recognising humanitarian realities. We cannot protect national security without knowing precisely who is within our borders,” he said, adding that accurate data would enable more effective management of issues ranging from security and law enforcement to public health, employment and community welfare.

items.title: Chow: Penang Pakatan wants more women in the race but finding willing candidates still proves tricky
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 15 — Penang Pakatan Harapan (PH) hopes to field more women candidates in the next state election, although identifying a sufficient number of suitable and willing candidates remains a challenge, said its chairman Chow Kon Yeow. He said Penang PH remained committed to the target of achieving at least 30 per cent women’s participation in politics and decision-making, but continued to face difficulties finding enough suitable women willing to contest. In every election, we will field women candidates, but the actual number will depend on whether there are enough suitable and willing candidates.

items.title: Higher Education D-G says universities must deliver tangible impact, not just achievements that look good on paper
items.summary: Higher Education director-general Datuk Prof Dr Azlinda Azman said IPTAs today cannot focus solely on academic achievements. Rather, every ringgit allocated to these institutions must be translated into tangible impact on national development and the global community. “IPTAs must act as engines of national growth, centres of social reconciliation and dynamic problem-solvers for communities.

items.title: ‘Prepared to defend my innocence’: Tony Pua says likely to be charged over Rukun Negara post
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Former Damansara MP Tony Pua said today he is likely to be charged in court over his Facebook post regarding the Rukun Negara, after police recorded an additional statement from him at Bukit Aman this morning. Pua said he was called in for further questioning after the investigation papers were returned by the Attorney General’s Chambers to Bukit Aman for additional questions. Pua said the additional questions mostly concerned the definition of the word “decree” and other matters linked to his Facebook post.

items.title: Banks scrap RM1 interbank ATM fee from July, giving Malaysians unlimited free withdrawals nationwide
items.summary: The initiative allows Malaysian debit cardholders to access over 14,000 ATMs and SRMs nationwide, regardless of their issuing bank, with unlimited free cash withdrawals, according to a joint statement from three financial services industry associations today. The statement was issued by the Association of Banks in Malaysia (ABM), the Association of Islamic Banking and Financial Institutions Malaysia (AIBIM), and the Association of Development Finance Institutions of Malaysia (ADFIM). “This initiative allows Malaysian debit cardholders to access over 14,000 ATMs and SRMs nationwide, regardless of their issuing bank, with unlimited free cash withdrawals,” they said, adding that the waiver applies to ATMs and SRMs operated by banks in Malaysia.

items.title: Police gear up for Johor, Negeri Sembilan elections, says IGP
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Police are preparing to deploy additional personnel and resources to ensure the upcoming Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections proceed smoothly, Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail said today. He said Johor alone is expected to require between 4,000 and 5,000 additional police personnel. “Johor is a large state with about 56 state seats being contested.

items.title: Reports of Kuching–Bangkok slots premature, AirBorneo says focus remains on intra‑Borneo recovery
items.summary: KUCHING, June 15 — AirBorneo has clarified that it has not submitted an application for a direct Kuching-Bangkok service and that no such route has been formally confirmed at this stage. In a statement issued to The Borneo Post , AirBorneo said while destinations across the region form part of its longer-term network planning, no application has been submitted for the recently mentioned Bangkok route. “However, we wish to clarify that no application has been submitted for the recently mentioned Bangkok route at this stage, as nothing has been formally confirmed,” the airline said.

items.title: Police bust mini casino, online gambling operation in Bukit Jalil luxury condo, arrest 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Police have uncovered a gambling syndicate operating a mini casino and online gambling activities from a luxury residential unit in Bukit Jalil following a raid on Saturday. Cheras deputy police chief Supt Lim Chun How said the district police headquarters’ Anti-Vice, Gambling and Secret Societies Division (D7) busted the operation during Op Noda/Dadu, reported Harian Metro . Police also seized various gambling equipment, including a Texas Hold’em casino table, hundreds of gambling chips, playing cards, RM4,904 in cash, laptops and a karaoke audio system.

items.title: Parliament set for fiery debate as Economy Minister tackles energy crunch and supply chain shocks
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — The Ministry of Economy is set to deliver a ministerial statement addressing the global energy crisis and worldwide supply chain disruptions when Parliament reconvenes on June 22, Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir confirmed. He said the statement would include a debate session to allow MPs to discuss the government’s actions and plans in addressing the impact of the crisis on the national economy. Akmal Nasrullah said that the government, through the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), has increased its meeting frequency from six to 12 times a year due to pressures from the global crisis, with 124 decisions recorded so far.

items.title: Five Japanese men in Penang scam probe unable to enter plea as language hurdle delays court proceedings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Proceedings against five Japanese men linked to an international scam syndicate were delayed at the Magistrates’ Court in Bukit Mertajam today after language barriers prevented charges from being read out. The court also denied bail for all five accused. Earlier reports said police in Penang had dismantled three international scam syndicates in separate raids on call centre premises around Bukit Mertajam earlier this month.

items.title: Muda, PSM form Progressive Bloc for Johor and N. Sembilan state polls
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) and the Socialist Party of Malaysia (PSM) have agreed to form a political collaboration known as the Progressive Bloc ahead of the Johor and Negri Sembilan state elections, according to a joint media statement. The statement said both parties had reached a consensus to work together throughout the election campaign period. According to the joint press release by Muda and PSM, the Progressive Bloc will focus on presenting progressive policy proposals to voters in both states.

items.title: DBKL invites landowners and public to propose changes to Kuala Lumpur Local Plan 2040
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) is inviting registered landowners, developers and members of the public to submit applications for amendments to the Kuala Lumpur Local Plan 2040 (PTKL2040), which is currently in force as the capital’s primary development reference document. Kuala Lumpur Mayor Datuk Seri Fadlun Mak Ujud said interested parties must submit a Letter of Intent (LOI) to DBKL’s City Planning Department (JPRB) by 5pm on July 10. Fadlun said the Manual for the Kuala Lumpur Local Plan Amendment Proposal Report will be available through the Kuala Lumpur Development Plan portal from today.

items.title: N. Sembilan MB’s press secretary apologises to Undang rulers over Anthony Loke ‘Tok Undang’ remark
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 15 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar’s press secretary Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah today apologised to the Undang Yang Empat over his use of a title associated with the Undang institution during the launch of Pakatan Harapan’s (PH) Negeri Sembilan election machinery on Friday. Ahmad Shahir said that as a Negeri Sembilan native who holds the state’s customs, culture and the Undang institution in high regard, he viewed the use of the title “Dato’ Undang” seriously as it involved the sensitivities of the Adat Perpatih institution, which should be respected by all parties regardless of political affiliation. He said PH remained committed to respecting and upholding the dignity of the Undang institution as a valuable heritage that forms part of Negeri Sembilan’s identity and pride.

items.title: Appellate court gives prosecution more time to serve appeal notice on Yusoff Rawther over drug, fake gun acquittal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 15 — The Court of Appeal has granted the prosecution more time to locate former research assistant Yusoff Rawther and personally serve him with a notice of appeal against his acquittal on drug trafficking and imitation firearms charges. According to Free Malaysia Today, the prosecution had sought an extension after failing to effect personal service of the notice of appeal on Yusoff, the respondent in the case. “Under Section 314 of the Criminal Procedure Code, the appellant (prosecution) must serve the notice personally on the respondent,” he said.

items.title: Malaysia must boost media literacy as AI reshapes information landscape, says deputy communications minister
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 15 — Media literacy among the public needs to be strengthened so that people are better equipped to critically evaluate information and identify misleading content amid the rapid evolution of the digital media landscape, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said. She said the ability to distinguish between credible information and manipulated content has become increasingly important as society enters a new era driven by generative artificial intelligence (AI), which is reshaping the global media ecosystem. Teo said efforts to strengthen media literacy should be complemented by greater training in AI, digital verification and information security to ensure Malaysian journalists are well prepared for future challenges.
